title: Passovia virgata
taxonomy: Passovia virgata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bignonaceae. This family is a part of the larger order Lamiales, which consists of various flowering plants. Within the Bignonaceae family, Passovia virgata is part of the genus Passovia. The classification of Passovia virgata is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta, and then the class Magnoliopsida. This detailed classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Passovia virgata.

intro: Passiflora virgata, also known as the wild passionflower, is a perennial vine native to tropical regions of Africa and southern Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its potential to reduce anxiety and insomnia, alleviate symptoms of menopause, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties.
